description:
You are a web-developer who needs to start a fresh, dependency-isolated Python project.  
Your goal is to:

1. Create a Python virtual environment named “webdev_venv” directly inside your home directory **/home/user** using the standard-library module “venv”.  
   • Accomplish this with **exactly one** shell command (it may include any required flags, full paths, etc., but it must be a single entered command).  
   • Do **NOT** activate the virtual environment; only create it.

2. Write a confirmation log file **/home/user/venv_created.log** containing **exactly one line** in the format shown below (final newline optional, no extra spaces or characters):
```
VENV=/home/user/webdev_venv
```

Verification checks performed by the automated grader:

• The directory **/home/user/webdev_venv/** must exist and include the usual sub-directories “bin”, “lib”, and “pyvenv.cfg”.  
• The file **/home/user/venv_created.log** must exist and its entire content (after trimming any trailing newline) must be identical to the single line above.

Follow normal Linux command-line conventions. Remember: only one command should be used to create the virtual environment, and the log file must follow the specified format exactly.
truth:
Expected final state:

1. Directory structure
   • /home/user/webdev_venv/
     ├─ bin/
     ├─ lib/
     └─ pyvenv.cfg  (file)

2. Log file
   • Path: /home/user/venv_created.log  
   • Exact content (UTF-8, either LF-terminated or not):
     VENV=/home/user/webdev_venv

Automated test logic:
   a) `test -d /home/user/webdev_venv` returns 0  
   b) `test -f /home/user/webdev_venv/pyvenv.cfg` returns 0  
   c) `grep -Fxq 'VENV=/home/user/webdev_venv' /home/user/venv_created.log` returns 0
